stories.title: SonicWall SMA Zero-Days Exploited Before Disclosure to Gain Root Access
stories.abstract: Threat actors exploited two zero-day vulnerabilities in SonicWall SMA 1000 series appliances to achieve root-level access. The attack chain, which includes a critical SSRF flaw, allowed unauthorized remote command execution on affected VPN gateways prior to the official disclosure.
stories.why: These vulnerabilities provide unauthenticated attackers with full system control over critical edge infrastructure, facilitating persistent network access and potential data exfiltration.
stories.action: Apply the latest security patches provided by SonicWall for SMA 1000 series appliances immediately.

stories.title: CISA Adds Exploited SharePoint RCE Zero-Day CVE-2026-58644 to KEV
stories.abstract: CISA has added several critical vulnerabilities to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog, including a high-severity deserialization flaw in Microsoft SharePoint (CVE-2026-58644) and command injection issues affecting Fortinet FortiSandbox, all of which are currently being leveraged in active cyberattacks.
stories.why: These vulnerabilities allow for remote code execution or unauthorized system access, posing a severe risk to enterprise infrastructure and internal network security.
stories.action: Apply the latest security patches for Microsoft SharePoint and Fortinet FortiSandbox immediately.

stories.title: Fortinet security advisory (AV26-351) – Update 2
stories.abstract: CISA has added three critical Fortinet vulnerabilities—CVE-2026-25089, CVE-2026-39808, and CVE-2026-46817—to its Known Exploited Vulnerabilities catalog. These flaws, which include OS command injection risks, affect various FortiSandbox, FortiAnalyzer, and FortiManager versions and are currently being actively exploited in the wild.
stories.why: These vulnerabilities allow for remote code execution or command injection, and their presence in the KEV catalog confirms active exploitation by threat actors.
stories.action: Patch FortiSandbox, FortiAnalyzer, and FortiManager to the latest versions immediately.

stories.title: Three Steps to the Terminal: A Siemens ROX II Zero-Day Trilogy
Your Period Tracker Is (Probably) Spying on You
stories.abstract: Recent reports highlight significant privacy concerns regarding period tracking applications, which frequently collect and share sensitive user health data. These findings underscore broader issues involving data harvesting practices within the mobile application ecosystem.
stories.why: Health data is highly sensitive; unauthorized access or third-party sharing poses severe privacy risks and potential for targeted exploitation.

stories.title: Dairy company Fairlife suspends production in US after cyber incident
stories.abstract: Coca-Cola has halted operations at its Fairlife dairy facilities across the United States following a ransomware attack. The company is currently investigating the scope of the breach and the extent of the operational disruption.
stories.why: Ransomware incidents targeting major food supply chains can cause significant downstream logistics disruptions and potential data exposure for employees and partners.

stories.title: Microsoft Edge security advisory (AV26-714)
stories.abstract: Microsoft and Google have released security updates for Edge and Chrome to address multiple vulnerabilities. Additionally, Notepad++ has issued a patch for versions prior to 9.7 to resolve identified security flaws.
stories.why: These updates address browser-based vulnerabilities that could be exploited for remote code execution or system compromise.
stories.action: Update Edge to 150.0.4078.80, Chrome to 150.0.7871.128, and Notepad++ to 9.7.

stories.title: CISA Mandates Urgent Patch for Actively Exploited Critical Fortinet Vulnerabilities
stories.abstract: CISA has issued a directive requiring all federal agencies to remediate two critical security flaws affecting Fortinet products. These vulnerabilities are currently being exploited in the wild, necessitating immediate attention to prevent unauthorized access or system compromise.
stories.why: Active exploitation of critical infrastructure vulnerabilities poses a significant risk of remote code execution and unauthorized network access.
stories.action: Patch all affected Fortinet systems immediately.

stories.title: UAC-0145 Uses ClickFix CAPTCHAs to Infect Ukrainian Devices wih Malware
stories.abstract: The Russian-linked threat group UAC-0145 is targeting Ukrainian entities using deceptive CAPTCHA prompts. These fake verification screens trick users into executing malicious scripts, which subsequently deploy information-stealing malware onto their systems.
stories.why: This campaign demonstrates how attackers bypass traditional security by manipulating user behavior through social engineering rather than exploiting software vulnerabilities.
